module_map.responsibility: Entry point orchestration - coordinates metadata fetching, sorting, and display phases
File metadata collection and calculation - resolves permissions, dates, sizes, ownership, git status, symlink targets
Grid/tree rendering engine - formats Meta into terminal output with padding, colors, icons
key_functions: inner_display_grid(display_option, metas, owner_cache, flags, colors, icons, git_theme, depth, term_width)
get_output(meta, owner_cache, colors, icons, git_theme, flags, display_option, padding_rules, tree)
inner_display_tree(metas, owner_cache, flags, colors, icons, git_theme, tree_depth_prefix, padding_rules, tree_index)

module_map.responsibility: CLI parsing and configuration - defines displayable columns (blocks), layout options, sorting rules, icons mode, colors, dates
key_types: Block enum (Size, Date, Name, Owner, Permissions, GitStatus, etc.)

module_map.responsibility: Visual rendering configurations - icon theme selection, color schemes, git decorations
Sorting strategies - implements multiple sort criteria (size, date, name) with direction control
Git repository integration - fetches file status (staged, modified, untracked, etc.)

data_flow.processing: For each path, resolves: file type, permissions, size, date, owner, symlink target, git status (if repo), name/display formatting
data_flow.notes: Single path -> one Meta. Recursion handled separately via Meta::recurse_into with depth tracking

data_flow.processing: Applies Sorter enum (Size/Date/Name/Version/Extension) based on Flags::sorting config
data_flow.notes: Sort criteria come from flags.sorting (parsed from CLI). Stable sort preserves secondary order.

data_flow.processing: Matches file name/extension against theme icon mappings; tty detection; fallback to defaults
data_flow.integration: Called inside get_output() for each row; icon prepended to file name in output

data_flow.processing: Applies color rules: file type (dir/symlink/exe), permissions (suid/sticky), git status (modified/staged), LS_COLORS override
data_flow.integration: Colorized strings created via Colors::colorize(input, elem) for each block value

data_flow.input: Meta, Flags (blocks config), padding rules, padding_rules: HashMap<Block, usize>
data_flow.key_function: get_output(meta, owner_cache, colors, icons, git_theme, flags, display_option, padding_rules, tree)
data_flow.processing: For each Block in Flags::blocks (Size, Date, Owner, Permissions, Name, GitStatus, etc.), render Meta field with color/icon/padding
types_consumed: Meta fields (size, date, owner, permissions, name, etc.)
data_flow.integration: Blocks control which columns appear and their order; padding_rules align columns

data_flow.key_function: inner_display_grid(...) for grid, inner_display_tree(...) for tree
data_flow.processing: Grid: compute column widths, build header, align columns; Tree: recursive indentation, tree chars (├──), nesting
data_flow.integration: Grid padding from Blocks; tree prefix logic from recursion depth; output sent to stdout

cross_module_hubs.reason: Central rendering hub: imports from core (Flags, Meta), color (Colors), icon (Icons), git_theme (GitTheme), theme (ColorTheme, Icons). Called by core::display(); calls get_output (25 functions) and inner_display_* functions. Coordinates all visual transformations.

Data source hub: core::fetch() calls Meta::from_path(); display passes Meta to all rendering functions; sort operates on Vec<Meta>. Imports from all meta/* submodules (name, permissions, date, size, owner, filetype, symlink, git_file_status, etc.). Central data container.
dependents: core.rs (fetch calls from_path, sort, display consume Meta)

cross_module_hubs.reason: Configuration hub: core imports Flags; display::get_output iterates Flags.blocks; sort uses Flags.sorting; icon/color use Flags for mode selection. Blocks enum defines which Meta fields appear in output; sorting rules, icon modes, color modes all configured here.
dependents: core.rs (Flags parsed, passed to all phases)

files_to_modify: src/meta/mod.rs (add checksum field to Meta struct)
src/flags/mod.rs (export new checksum module, add --checksum CLI flag)
Cargo.toml (add sha2 or similar crate for SHA-256)
new_types: meta::Checksum: struct { hash: String, computed: bool } (lazy compute on demand, cache result)
Block::Checksum: enum variant in flags/blocks.rs (add to Blocks vector, get_header(), try_from())
MetaOptions or ChecksumOption: enum(Always, Never, OnDemand) to control when checksum computed (large files may be slow)
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow meta/size.rs (lazy calculation) and meta/git_file_status.rs (optional, depends on cache context). Checksum should be computed once, cached in Meta. In display.rs, follow the pattern in get_output() where each Block variant maps to a Meta field accessor, e.g., Block::Size => meta.size.get_short_string(); add Block::Checksum => meta.checksum.hash.clone() (or formatted string).
change_proposal.integration_point: 1. Meta::from_path() calls checksum module (e.g., meta::checksum::Checksum::from_path(path, options)) - but LAZY to avoid blocking on large files. 2. In display::get_output(), add Block::Checksum => { render checksum string with color }. 3. Flags::blocks must support Block::Checksum in configuration (TOML config file and CLI). 4. Check if theme/color.rs needs Elem::Checksum variant for color mapping. 5. Update flags/blocks.rs get_header() to include 'Checksum' column header.
risks: Performance: SHA-256 hashing large files blocks display; mitigate with lazy compute (compute only if Block::Checksum in --blocks), or async/background thread
Configuration complexity: --blocks now must accept 'checksum'; update parser and validation
Cache invalidation: if file modified, cached checksum stale; consider mtime tracking
Cross-platform: Windows may need alternate hash library; verify sha2 crate compatibility
Column width: hash string is 64 chars (SHA-256 hex); may cause terminal wrap; consider truncation option (like truncate_owner flag)

summary.analysis_approach: Used code-analyze-mcp to trace architecture without reading full source. Mode sequence: overview (structure) → file_details (key modules) → symbol_focus (data flow).
summary.key_findings: Three-phase pipeline: (1) core::fetch() collects Meta via meta::from_path() for each file/dir, (2) core::sort() applies sorting from Flags, (3) core::display() renders via display::inner_display_grid/tree(). Data flows through Meta struct (central container) carrying all file attributes. Display coordinates with themes (Icons, Colors) and flags (Blocks, Layout). Cross-module hubs: display.rs (visual rendering), meta/mod.rs (data source), flags/ (configuration). Adding checksum requires: lazy compute in Meta, new Block variant in flags/blocks.rs, checksum rendering in display::get_output(), theme color mapping.
